title: CS17
characteristics_ch1: tissue: amnion | cell type: amnion cells
extract_protocol_ch1: All tissues for sequencing and spatial work were collected in HypoThermosol FRS preservation solution (H4416-100ML Merck) and stored at 4 °C until processing. Tissue dissociation was conducted within 24 h of tissue retrieval.Tissues were cut into <1 mm3 segments before being digested with Trypsin/EDTA 0.25% phenol red (25200072 Thermo Fisher Scientific) for 10-15 min at 37 °C with intermittent shaking. Digested tissue was passed through a 100 µm filter and cells collected by centrifugation (500g for 5 min at 4 °C). Cells were washed with PBS and then resuspended in PBS 0.04% BSA before cell counting. | cells were loaded according to the manufacturer’s protocol for the Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 5 v2 (Dual index) kit for CS17 amnion and Chromium Next GEM Single Cell 3 v3.1 (Dual index) kit for CS19 and CS22 amnion from 10X Genomics to attain 7,000 cells per reaction. Library preparation was carried out according to the manufacturer’s protocol. Libraries were sequenced, aiming at a minimum coverage of 20,000 raw reads per cell, on the Illumina HiSeq4000 or Novaseq 6000 systems using the sequencing format: read 1, 26 cycles; i7 index, 8 cycles, i5 index, 0 cycles; read 2, 98 cycles.
data_processing: Cell Ranger software from 10X Genomics was installed for data pre-processing. Raw sequencing data were organized, with the requirement that sequencing reads be demultiplexed into FASTQ format files for each sample. Using cellranger mkfastq to demultiplex raw base call (BCL) files generated by Illumina sequencers into FASTQ files. Reads were mapped to the human hg38 genome and counted with GRCh38-3.0.0 annotation using cellranger count. The data pre-processing workflow was streamlined and standardized to maintain consistency across samples. | Assembly: hg38,GRCh38-3.0.0 | Supplementary files format and content: Tab-separated values files and matrix files
